paper_focus.title: Tailoring Strength and Ductility of an Fe–Mn–Al–C Low-Density Duplex Steel by Controlling the Cooling Path after Hot Rolling
paper_focus.research_objective: To elucidate how different cooling paths after hot rolling affect the microstructural evolution and mechanical properties, especially strength-ductility balance, in an Fe-Mn-Al-C low-density duplex steel.
main_process_variables: hot rolling temperature range 900–1100 °C, seven passes to 4 mm sheet
homogenization at 1050 °C for 2 h, reheating at 1200 °C for 2 h
cooling rate after hot rolling: air cooling (AC) vs. furnace cooling (FC)
expected_information_axis: microstructure (phase fractions, grain size, morphology, KAM/GND density)
property (tensile stress-strain curves, work hardening rate, strength-ductility match, fracture behavior)
mechanism (austenite stability, TRIP effect, martensite cracking, delamination of banded structure)
